commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=77e4cc01149b1fb28b94213a804128ca5e37af0b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Unix process-tree termination to direct-PID kills by default, requires `detached: true` for process-group kills, updates one exec timeout caller, and adjusts kill-tree tests.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +35. Total +37 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main defaults omitted detachment to group kill, and the PR flips that default while detached production callers still omit the option. I did not run a live macOS gateway or real MCP process-tree reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action needs author or maintainer follow-up because the patch has a shared-helper regression and the external PR proof is mock-only, so automation should not replace the contributor’s real setup proof.

target.title: fix(process): default killProcessTree to direct-pid kill, opt-in group-kill via detached:true (fixes #76259)
target.body:
## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `killProcessTree` defaults to Unix group-kill (`process.kill(-pid, ...)`) which sends SIGTERM to the caller's own process group when the child was not spawned detached. This kills the gateway on macOS when a timed-out agent CLI child shares the gateway's process group.
- **Environment tested:** macOS Darwin 26.4.1, node v24, OpenClaw local build from upstream/main
- **Steps run after the patch:**
  1. Applied the two-line default-swap in `packages/agent-core/src/harness/env/kill-tree.ts` (`opts?.detached !== false` → `opts?.detached === true`) and added `detached: true` to the `terminateProcessTree` call in `src/process/exec.ts:501`
  2. Ran `kill-tree.test.ts` (13 tests) and `exec.test.ts` (12 tests)
  3. Ran `pnpm build` to verify compilation

- **Evidence after fix:**

```
$ grep -n 'useGroupKill' packages/agent-core/src/harness/env/kill-tree.ts
40:  const useGroupKill = opts?.detached === true;

$ grep -n 'terminateProcessTree' src/process/exec.ts
501:        terminateProcessTree(child.pid, { graceMs: COMMAND_PROCESS_TREE_KILL_GRACE_MS, detached: true });

$ npx vitest run src/process/kill-tree.test.ts src/process/exec.test.ts
 ✓  process  src/process/kill-tree.test.ts (13 tests) 12ms
 ✓  process  src/process/exec.test.ts (12 tests) 147ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  25 passed (25)

$ pnpm build
✓ built in 506ms
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `killProcessTree` now defaults to direct `process.kill(pid, ...)` — safe when the child shares the caller's process group. Group-kill (`process.kill(-pid, ...)`) is opt-in via `detached: true`, which `exec.ts` passes explicitly when the child was spawned detached. All 25 tests pass. Build succeeds.
- **What was not tested:** Live macOS gateway restart scenario (requires a running gateway + Telegram dispatch). The test suite validates the signal-targeting logic with mocked `process.kill`.
target.author: liuhao1024
